description_long: This is a robust, traditionally crafted ceramic vase with a rounded, bulbous body and a narrow, slightly flared neck. The surface displays a dark, earthy coloration with visible patches of lighter wear and some minor surface imperfections, suggesting age and use. Four small, rounded lugs are evenly spaced around the shoulder, possibly for decorative or functional purposes such as tying or carrying. The lower portion of the vase transitions to a reddish-brown tone, indicating a different clay composition or firing effect. The mouth is wide enough for inserting medium-sized floral arrangements or other decorative items. The overall finish is matte and rough, with a tactile, hand-made quality typical of utilitarian pottery found in urban parks or public spaces.
description: A dark, rounded ceramic vase with a narrow neck, four small lugs, and a matte, weathered finish. The lower section is reddish-brown, showing signs of age and use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the vase's wide body, narrow neck, and two prominent lugs on the shoulder.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the transition between the dark upper and reddish-brown lower sections, with all four lugs partially visible.
description_view_2: The vase appears darker overall, with the neck and lugs less distinct due to lighting and shadow.
description_view_3: A side view emphasizing the rounded form and the subtle texture of the surface, with two lugs clearly visible.
